cd 0.3.0

Aggregate monthly rasters to seasonal and annual periods

Source: R/cd_aggregate.R
cd_aggregate.Rd

Takes a multi-band raster with one band per month and aggregates to annual and seasonal periods. Season definitions are configurable.

Usage

cd_aggregate(x, method = "mean", seasons = cd_seasons())

Arguments

x

A terra::SpatRaster with 12 bands (one per month, Jan through Dec).

method

Character. Aggregation method: "mean" or "sum". Default "mean". Use "sum" for precipitation.

seasons

Named list of integer vectors defining month groups. Default uses standard meteorological seasons: winter (DJF), spring (MAM), summer (JJA), fall (SON).

Value

A named list of terra::SpatRaster objects, one per period. Names are "annual", plus the names from seasons.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# 12-band raster (one per month)
r <- terra::rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, nlyrs = 12)
terra::values(r) <- matrix(rnorm(1200), 100, 12)

# Default seasons
periods <- cd_aggregate(r)
names(periods)  # "annual", "winter", "spring", "summer", "fall"

# Custom seasons (e.g., wet/dry)
cd_aggregate(r, seasons = list(wet = 10:3, dry = 4:9))
} # }
